data.narrative:
Neuralink, a private-stage AI & Software company founded by Elon Musk and others, is currently valued at $9.7B following $1.3B+ in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 9 comparables: 8 private companies and 1 public (Palantir). Primary private peers include OpenAI ($300.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), Anysphere ($29.3B), and Groq ($7.0B).

Peer-median valuation represents the applicable triangulation method given data availability. The median valuation across the 4 same-sector, same-stage peers is $106.2B, with a range spanning $7.0B to $300.0B. This dispersion reflects significant uncertainty within the AI & Software private market, particularly the valuation variance between infrastructure-focused and large language model companies. The peer-median method implies a valuation of $106.2B.

The triangulated valuation range is $90.2B (low) to $122.1B (high), centered on $106.2B. This assessment rests on a single methodological leg—peer-median analysis—which limits conviction. The current $9.7B valuation sits substantially below the implied range, suggesting either a valuation lag relative to sector comparables or material execution risk not captured in peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the company's stage of commercial validation, capital requirements to reach profitability, and technology risk relative to established competitors in the AI space.
